business_summary: Alphabet is the parent of Google. It makes money in three segments: (1) Google Services ($95.9B in Q4 2025; ~$354B for FY2025) - the cash engine, driven by Search advertising, YouTube ads and subscriptions, Android, Chrome, Google Play, and the Google subscriptions/platforms/devices line (Google One, YouTube Premium; over 325M paid consumer subscriptions). (2) Google Cloud ($17.7B in Q4 2025, +48% YoY, now over a $70B annual run rate) - infrastructure (GCP), Workspace, and enterprise AI products; a fast-growing, increasingly profitable segment ($5.3B Q4 operating income). (3) Other Bets (Waymo and other ventures; small and loss-making). FY2025 total revenue was $402.8B, up 15%, the first year above $400B. The business should still be winning in 5-10 years because Google Search has a dominant share of the world's information queries with a powerful data/distribution feedback loop, YouTube is the leading video platform, Android/Chrome control key distribution, and Alphabet owns full-stack AI (Gemini models, TPUs, and a global data-center footprint) to defend Search and grow Cloud.

moat.type: Network effects (Search/YouTube/Android), scale economies, unmatched proprietary data, distribution control (Chrome/Android), brand, and full-stack AI infrastructure (Gemini + custom TPUs)

quality.gross_margin: 59.7% (FY2025: revenue $402.836B less cost of revenues $162.535B = gross profit $240.301B)
quality.roic: high; ROE ~35.7% on average equity (FY2025 net income $132.170B vs avg equity ~$370B). Note GAAP net income is inflated by ~$24.6B pre-tax non-operating gains on equity securities; on a normalized basis ROE is still ~30%.
quality.net_debt_to_ebitda: net cash (negative net debt): cash+marketable securities $126.843B vs long-term debt $46.547B = ~$80.3B net cash; debt/equity ~0.11
quality.fcf_positive: yes, but compressed by AI capex: FY2025 FCF ~$73.27B (OCF $164.713B - capex $91.447B). Capex nearly doubled YoY ($52.5B->$91.4B) and 2026 guidance is $175-185B.

valuation.method: Blend via code/valuation.py of pe (normalized diluted EPS $9.13 x 24/28/32) and fcf_yield (FCF/share $5.99 at 4.0/3.5/3.0%). EPS normalized by stripping ~$20.5B after-tax non-operating gains on equity securities from GAAP net income (GAAP diluted EPS was $10.81). The FCF method is intentionally harsh because FY2025 capex ($91.4B) is at a cyclical AI-buildout peak; if AI capex earns its cost of capital, normalized FCF and fair value would be materially higher than this band.

bull_case: Wide, durable moat: Search has dominant query share with a data/distribution flywheel; YouTube leads online video; Android and Chrome control distribution. These are among the strongest moats in technology.
Exceptional economics: 59.7% gross margin, 32.0% operating margin, ~36% ROE on average equity, and a net-cash balance sheet (~$80B net cash).
Google Cloud is a real second growth engine: +48% to $17.7B in Q4 2025, now a >$70B run rate, and newly and durably profitable ($5.3B Q4 operating income).

Full-stack AI position: owns frontier Gemini models, custom TPUs, and global data centers; Gemini App reached over 750M monthly active users and management says Search usage is at record highs, suggesting AI is expanding (not cannibalizing) Search so far.
Strong, growing cash generation funds heavy AI investment while still buying back stock ($45.7B FY2025) and paying a dividend.
risks: Valuation is the dominant risk. At $367.93 the stock trades far above our blended fair-value band (base ~$213): roughly 40x normalized EPS and a capex-depressed ~3.8% FCF yield. The price already assumes years of strong growth and eventual capex normalization; there is no margin of safety today.
AI capex is exploding: FY2025 capex $91.4B and 2026 guidance $175-185B. If AI demand or returns disappoint, free cash flow stays depressed and ROIC falls. This is an unproven bet at enormous scale.
AI is also a threat to the core: chatbots and AI answer engines could erode the high-margin Search advertising model over time, even as Google races to defend it with its own AI.

Regulatory/antitrust overhang: Google lost the U.S. DOJ search-monopoly case and faces remedies (potential limits on default-placement deals, data sharing, or structural relief), plus separate ad-tech litigation and EU pressure. Outcomes could hit the most profitable parts of the business.
GAAP earnings quality: FY2025 net income is flattered by ~$24.6B of volatile mark-to-market gains on equity securities; reported EPS growth overstates core operating growth.
Dual-class voting structure concentrates control with founders; minority holders have limited say.
what_would_break_thesis: Google Services (advertising) revenue declines year-over-year for two consecutive quarters - the clearest sign AI is eroding the core Search franchise.
Total revenue growth falls below 8% for two consecutive quarters with no offsetting margin expansion.
Google Cloud revenue growth falls below 20% for two consecutive quarters (loss of the second growth engine).
Consolidated operating margin falls below 25% for two consecutive quarters (AI capex/opex not earning its keep).
Free cash flow turns negative for a full fiscal year, or net cash position flips to net debt with net-debt/EBITDA above 1.5x.
A regulatory remedy (DOJ search case or ad-tech case) that forces a structural break-up or materially cuts Search/ad-tech revenue or margin.
Any serious accounting restatement or governance red flag.
catalysts: Evidence that AI capex is earning its cost of capital: Cloud margins expanding and FCF re-accelerating as the buildout matures.
Continued Gemini/AI-Overviews adoption that grows Search engagement and monetization rather than cannibalizing it.
A price pullback toward the $245 high / $213 base fair-value zone, which would open a genuine margin of safety.
Favorable or contained outcome in the DOJ antitrust remedy phase.
